channel.title: Intellectual-History on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Intellectual-History on emsenn.net

item.title: Genealogy of Dialectics
item.description:
¶Orientation

A genealogy asks not “what is the final definition?” but “through what transformations did this practice become what it is now?” A genealogy of dialectics therefore tracks shifts in function, not only shifts in vocabulary.

The word “dialectic” persists across millennia, but its role changes:

  • from conversational testing,
  • to logical training,
  • to a theory of conceptual development,
  • to a method for analyzing historical-material contradiction.

The continuity is the concern with contradiction and revision. The breaks are about where contradiction lives and what dialectics is for.

item.title: History of Dialectics
item.description:
¶Scope

If the genealogy asks how dialectics came to be, this history asks how dialectics has been historically used, reworked, institutionalized, and contested.

The focus here is modern and contemporary circulation: from Hegel’s system, through Marxist transformation, into twentieth-century party, critical-theoretical, and anti-colonial formations.

¶1) Nineteenth century pivot: from speculative system to social critique

Hegel’s mature work made dialectics a comprehensive philosophical method for conceptual development [@hegel-dialectics-sep-2019]. By the later nineteenth century, Marx’s intervention shifted the center of gravity. Marx’s own formulation in the 1873 afterword is explicit: he inverts Hegel’s method by grounding movement in material social relations [@marx-capital-afterword-1873].
